channel.title: NYT > World > Middle East
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Supreme Court Expands Trump’s Power Over Immigration With TPS and Southern Border Decisions
item.description: The court handed President Trump victories in his push to rescind deportation protections for hundreds of thousands of people and turn away migrants at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:description: The Supreme Court’s 6-to-3 decision in two cases with broad implications divided along ideological lines, with the liberal justices dissenting.

item.title: Iran Strikes Ship in Strait of Hormuz, Undermining Efforts to Restore Traffic
item.description: The attack, confirmed by U.S. and Iranian officials, came after Tehran threatened to disrupt shipping in the waterway and as Washington sought regional support for its peace agreement.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis, Farnaz Fassihi, Jenny Gross and Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Oil tankers and cargo vessels off the coast of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday.

item.title: Iraq’s New Leader Faces Resistance Trying to Bring Militias Under State Control
item.description: The Trump administration has demanded that Iraqi leaders distance themselves from Iran and rein in Iran-linked militias that are outside of government control.
item.dc:creator: Raja Abdulrahim and Falih Hassan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Members of the Peace Brigades militia during a ceremony in the city of Samarra, Iraq, in June, marking their separation from the Sadrist movement and their integration into the Iraqi security forces.

item.title: In New York Election Results, More Evidence of Eroding Support for Israel
item.description: If the shift in public opinion continues, it could reshape one of the United States’ closest alliances.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski and Jennifer Medina
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Bing Guan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York campaigning with Claire Valdez. “We will stand up to the genocide,” Ms. Valdez told supporters after winning her Democratic House primary on Tuesday.

item.title: The Trump-Led Republican Party Is Softening on Iran
item.description: The president has sought to recast the Iranian government as he pursues a peace deal. But there are signs that a softening on Iran in the Republican Party goes well beyond him.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pro-government demonstration in Tehran last month.

item.title: Bahrain Restricts Shiite Holy Day Amid Iran War Crackdown
item.description: The restrictions are the latest in a string of measures Bahrain has taken against Shiite citizens in the Gulf country, with many accused of having loyalties to Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Observing Ashura in the village of Sanabis, west of Manama, Bahrain, in 2015.

item.title: Israel Says It Has Trapped Hezbollah Fighters Underground in Southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military says thousands of troops have surrounded a large tunnel network in southern Lebanon. The standoff is at the center of intense clashes in the area recently.
item.dc:creator: David M. Halbfinger, Christina Goldbaum, Euan Ward and Samuel Granados

item.title: Rubio Seeks to Reassure Gulf Arab Partners on Iran Deal
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio was traveling among the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Bahrain, which were attacked by Iran during the war.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley, Edward Wong, Euan Ward and John Yoon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio greeting the Emirati ambassador to the U.S., Yousef al-Otaiba, in Abu Dhabi, U.A.E., on Tuesday.

item.title: U.S. Eases Travel Restrictions on Iran’s World Cup Team
item.description: The Iranians will be allowed to enter the United States two days, instead of one, before a pivotal game in Seattle on Friday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: The Iranian national team practicing in Carson, Calif., on Saturday. U.S. officials are letting the team enter the country two days before its match against Egypt in Seattle on Friday.

item.title: Trump Is Making Big Claims About the Iran Talks. Iran Keeps Contradicting Him.
item.description: President Trump appears to be describing his preferences as fully negotiated deals, in hopes of locking the Iranians in. The question is whether a succession of such disputes will sink the whole venture.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger and Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: In the negotiation between the United States and Iran, leaks are replaced by official, if fragmentary, announcements — usually from the American side.

item.title: How Shannon Manson, a Security Worker During the World Cup, Became Known in Jordan
item.description: Shannon Manson cheerily ushered a man from Jordan’s delegation away from fans. It turns out he was a prince, and now she is an unexpected internet sensation.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Prince Ali bin Hussein of Jordan, left, with the Jordanian national team’s captain, Ehsan Haddad in 2023.

item.title: U.N. Report Says Israeli Killings of Gaza Children Post-Truce Amount to Genocide
item.description: Israel’s U.N. mission dismissed the report as a “libelous sham,” and called the investigating commission a “fundamentally flawed mechanism.”
item.dc:creator: Nick Cumming-Bruce
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: A father carried the body of his 3‑year‑old, who was killed in an Israeli strike, according to medics, during his funeral in Gaza City in April.

item.title: In Middle East, Rubio Says ‘No Country’ Can Charge for Hormuz Traffic
item.description: The secretary of state dismissed Iran’s talk of charging ships to pass through the vital Persian Gulf portal as prohibited under international law.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley and Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke to reporters shortly after arriving in Abu Dhabi on Tuesday.

item.title: U.N. Sets Plan to Evacuate Stranded Ships Out of Persian Gulf
item.description: The International Maritime Organization, working with multiple countries, said it has ensured that vessels can sail safely through the Strait of Hormuz after months of being idled.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Hundreds of ships have been stranded in the Persian Gulf since the war began, waiting for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Israeli Soldiers Fatally Shoot 2 People in Southern Lebanon, Health Officials Say
item.description: The shooting came a day after Israeli troops began operating under new orders intended to reduce the risk of flare-ups in its conflict with Iran-backed Hezbollah militants.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Damaged buildings in Nabatieh, Lebanon, on Sunday. Israeli soldiers shot and killed two people near the city on Tuesday, Lebanese health officials said.

item.title: U.S. and Iran’s Conflicting Claims on Nuclear Inspection Show Challenge Ahead
item.description: President Trump said that in the latest round of peace talks, Iran had agreed to full inspection of its nuclear sites. Iran’s government said it had not.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands and Leily Nikounazar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: A satellite imagery taken on June 21, 2025, shows destroyed buildings at Isfahan nuclear site, Iran.

item.title: After Wars and False Starts, Cautious Optimism for U.S.-Iran Talks
item.description: Tehran and Washington face myriad negotiating pitfalls. But both sides are exhausted and running out of options. They may finally need a deal.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: In Tehran this month. Political analysts say both Iran and the United States now have a real need for progress.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes, Leo Sands and Nick Cumming-Bruce

item.title: Iranian Singer Sentenced to 74 Lashes for Performing Without Hijab
item.description: Parastoo Ahmadi and her band will also be barred from leaving the country or performing for two years, making clear to Iranians that their new leaders may be just as severe as those killed in the war.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin and Shirin Hakim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran Makes Moves to Assert Control Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: After Iran weaponized the waterway by making it too dangerous for businesses, experts say, the country is now looking to charge fees to vessels seeking to transit the vital water.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vessels anchored in the Strait of Hormuz, off the port city of Khasab on Oman’s northern Musandam Peninsula last month.

item.title: As Vance Leads Iran Negotiations, Trump Creates Disruptions in His Path
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is in a politically precarious spot.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: How Vice President JD Vance handles the future of the negotiations will factor into Republicans’ performance in the midterm elections and his future as a potential successor to President Trump.

item.title: Iran Team Leaves Locker Room Note: The ‘Spirit of Iran Remains Alive and Steadfast’
item.description: After fighting Belgium to a draw for one of Iran’s best World Cup results, the Iranians had a note for their fans and the world.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Iran national team has been allowed into the United States only for about 48 hours to play its games and then must leave the country.

item.title: U.S. Temporarily Lifts Oil Sanctions Against Iran, Citing ‘Productive’ Talks
item.description: President Trump and Vice President JD Vance pointed to progress on Iran’s nuclear program, but officials in Tehran said “no new commitments” had been made.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes, Rebecca F. Elliott, Alan Rappeport and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance after the United States and Iran held high-level talks at the Bürgenstock Resort in Switzerland on Monday.

item.title: Vance Claims Progress in First Day of Iran Talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance, the lead U.S. negotiator, said the two sides had agreed on technical issues and at least one substantive one, international inspection of Iranian nuclear sites, but an Iranian official said his country had made “no new commitments.”
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance spoke in Switzerland on Monday.

item.title: Vance Says Iran Will Allow Nuclear Watchdog to Restart Inspections
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Iran had agreed to invite experts from the U.N. agency to resume operations in the country. Tehran and the nuclear watchdog have not commented.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Tehran last week. Tensions have been high between Iran and the U.N. nuclear watchdog since the 12-day war last June.

item.title: U.S. Temporarily Lifts Sanctions on Iranian Oil
item.description: In a sharp reversal of American policy, the Treasury announced a 60-day reprieve, permitting the sale of Iranian crude as part of the preliminary U.S.-Iran deal toward ending the war.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport and Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Iran will be issued a license to sale of oil as part of the initial deal. The country will also reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: U.S. Oil Is Skipping the Chance to Grab Market Share From the Gulf
item.description: U.S. oil production is expected to grow only modestly next year as companies hesitate to spend more in an uncertain market.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smaller U.S. oil producers are starting to drill more wells, but big companies like Exxon Mobil and Chevron have said they are not changing their plans as a result of the war with Iran.

item.title: The Guardrails Designed to Protect U.S.-Iran Talks
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have agreed to set up a Strait of Hormuz hotline and a “de-confliction cell” for Lebanon, according to mediators from Pakistan and Qatar.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Chatham House (The Royal Institute of International Affairs)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan and Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman of Qatar at the Bürgenstock resort in Switzerland on Sunday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes, Alan Rappeport, Rebecca F. Elliott and Shirin Hakim

item.title: Explosion at Qatar Gas Plant Kills at Least 13 and Injures 66
item.description: The blast happened at a key site for natural gas production. The authorities blamed a technical malfunction while operations were being restarted after the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A screen grab from a video showing flames at the Ras Laffan industrial zone in Qatar on Sunday.

item.title: Uncertainty Remains Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States and Iranian militaries have offered conflicting accounts over whether the crucial waterway is open.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Strait of Hormuz continues to be a point of negotiation between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: As Vance Offers Iran ‘Outstretched Hand’ in Talks, Trump Threatens Bombing
item.description: The administration’s split message came as the United States and Iran began a new round of nuclear negotiations in Switzerland.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: From left, Steve Witkoff, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif of Pakistan, Vice President JD Vance and the Pakistani Army official Asim Munir at Lake Lucerne in Switzerland on Sunday.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Have Plenty to Negotiate Beyond Nuclear Issues
item.description: Both sides have committed to discussing sanctions relief and the shape of a reconstruction fund, as well as Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking at the Bürgenstock resort, Switzerland, on Sunday.

item.title: Netanyahu faces one of the biggest challenges of his career.
item.description: In a ‘plot twist’, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appears to be heading into an election this fall without his main prop: President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel during a news conference in Jerusalem this week.

item.title: Three Men Are Key to Iran’s Approach to U.S. Talks
item.description: Iran’s supreme leader, foreign minister and parliament speaker all play central roles in hammering out an agreement with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Abbas Araghchi, center, Iran’s foreign minister, arriving near Lucerne, Switzerland, on Sunday.

item.title: Israel Directs Its Military to Limit Its Actions in Lebanon, but Tensions Persist
item.description: It was not clear whether the new directive would resolve the friction that led to deadly clashes on Friday and Saturday and threatened to derail a preliminary U.S.-Iran peace deal.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Damage from Israeli strike in Nabatieh, Lebanon, on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Nuclear Talks Expected to Hinge on These 4 Questions
item.description: Tehran insists that its nuclear program is for peaceful purposes. The Trump administration is demanding assurances that Iran cannot secretly develop a weapon.
item.dc:creator: Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, on Sunday at the Bürgenstock resort in Switzerland.

item.title: A Glamorous Swiss Resort Hosts Officials for U.S.-Iran Talks
item.description: The Bürgenstock Resort Lake Lucerne has hosted some of the world’s wealthiest and most powerful people for more than 100 years.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Bürgenstock resort above Lake Lucerne in Switzerland has hosted some of the world’s wealthiest and most powerful people over nearly 150 years.

item.title: Neither the War Nor Trump’s Deal Terminated the Main Threats in Iran, Analysts Say
item.description: Neither the war nor the agreement terminated the main threats emanating from Iran, many analysts said.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A painting of Iran’s first two supreme leaders in Tehran this month. The second, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed at the start of the U.S.-Israeli campaign against Iran this year.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Ephrat Livni, Aurelien Breeden, Isabel Kershner and Yan Zhuang

item.title: U.S. and Iranian Officials to Meet for Peace Talks in Switzerland
item.description: Amid a volley of threats, the brief talks largely focused on the war in Lebanon, according to Iranian state media.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A plane carrying an Iranian negotiating team landed at Zurich International Airport in Switzerland on Saturday.

item.title: Mona Khalil, Defender of Sea Turtles, Killed in an Israeli Strike in Lebanon
item.description: For a quarter century, Ms. Khalil ran a guesthouse and worked to protect endangered sea turtles who every summer lay their eggs on a stretch of beach near Tyre, Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Ishani Desai
item.category: Society for the Protection of Nature in Lebanon
item.media:description: Mona Khalil in Mansuri, Lebanon, in 2004.

item.title: Canada’s Postcard Moment at the World Cup Is More Than the Team’s Victory
item.description: A devastating injury to midfielder Ismaël Koné showed the squad’s spirit.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.media:description: Nathan Saliba, a player with Canada’s team who replaced the midfielder Ismaël Koné after his injury, held up a team jersey with his teammate’s surname and number, after scoring a goal.

item.title: New Strait of Hormuz Closure Announcement Threatens the Slow Uptick in Traffic
item.description: Fifty-five ships had passed through the strait on Saturday, the U.S. military said. But then Iran’s military said it was closing the waterway once again.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar and Jonathan Wolfe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah Are Still Fighting, Several Cease-Fires Later
item.description: For the second time in two days, Lebanon was again at the center of whether the fragile U.S.-Iran deal would hold.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Rescue workers at the site of an Israeli airstrike in Qannarit village, southern Lebanon, on Saturday.

item.title: Shipping remains far below prewar levels in the Strait of Hormuz.
item.description: A preliminary U.S.-Iran deal took steps to reopen the vital waterway, but traffic remains erratic.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:description: Cargo vessels anchored off Muscat, Oman, on Saturday.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir, Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Alan Yuhas
